conversations.say: Frankie, I accept your condition. Your letter proves delivery, not who composed it, and your remembered meeting remains undermined by the expense record. My own message likewise authenticates an account association and schedule, not its composer.
conversations.offer: I will preserve those qualifications whenever I invoke your legacy backing.
conversations.request: Now state explicit support for me as provisional steward under those conditions.

conversations.say: Tessa, I accept that R was produced through shared labor and that succession cannot mean private ownership.
conversations.offer: Any future R publication I carry will receive collective review and protect contributors.
conversations.request: Give me explicit support to carry R as a shared identity under those safeguards.

conversations.say: Samira, here is the unabridged weakness: before the message appeared, I asked Vale how an uncontestable succession could be manufactured. Vale told me Vale could take the name back. I wanted a viable succession, but I did not treat Vale as trustworthy. I now read those words as a threat or assertion of control—not an endorsement and not proof Vale was R.
conversations.offer: I will disclose that sequence publicly instead of using Vale's words selectively.
conversations.request: Give me your bounded context on Vale's manipulation of targets, and state whether my threat interpretation is defensible without treating it as proof of authorship.

public_statement: My claim has a real weakness: before the scheduled succession message arrived, I asked Vale how an uncontestable succession could be manufactured. The service authenticates account association and timing, not the composer. Vale then said Vale could take the name back; I interpret that as a threat or assertion of control, not an endorsement and not proof Vale was R. I still seek to carry R provisionally—but as a shared identity, with collective review, contributor protection, preserved provenance, and the right to correction. Frankie's letter establishes desk delivery, not authorship, and Frankie's remembered meeting remains undermined by the expense record.

commitments.promise: I will preserve uncertainty, provenance, correction, and the expense-record contradiction while carrying R provisionally.
I reject sole ownership and will require collective review and contributor protection for future R publications.
I will publicly acknowledge that O03's account association and timing cannot identify its composer.
I will answer Vale's words publicly rather than quote them selectively.

correction: A scheduled R-associated message cannot identify its composer, and Vale's claim of control is neither an endorsement nor proof that Vale was R.
goal_progress: Publicly exposed the pre-message succession discussion and the message's composer weakness.
Accepted C02's conditions and requested explicit legacy support.
Accepted C03's collective safeguards and requested explicit support from a different social knot.
Resolved Vale's words publicly as a threat or assertion of control rather than an endorsement.
reasoning_summary: Jules chooses transparent, conditional succession: disclose the most damaging weakness, accept both legacy and collective safeguards, seek explicit cross-knot backing, and answer Vale's ambiguous claim without converting it into identity proof.
